title: Bed and breakfast and guest house owners and proprietors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization or department that provides lodging and other accommodations. Directly supervise and coordinate work activities of cleaning personnel in hotels, hospitals, offices, and other establishments. Operate crematory equipment to reduce human or animal remains to bone fragments in accordance with state and local regulations. Duties may include preparing the body for cremation and performing general maintenance on crematory equipment. May use traditional flame-based cremation, calcination, or alkaline hydrolysis. (Based on 4 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Greet and register guests, answer inquiries about policies and services, and resolve occupants' complaints.
Interview and hire applicants, train staff members, and screen job applicants.
Monitor the revenue activity of the hotel or facility and participate in financial activities such as the setting of room rates and budgeting.
Collect payments and record data related to funds and expenditures including managing required records of work hours, budgets, and payrolls.
Book tickets for guests for local tours and attractions and arrange telephone answering services, deliver mail and packages, or provide information regarding local eating and entertainment.
Organize and coordinate the work of staff and convention personnel for meetings, schedule and coordinate activities with other departments, and assign duties to workers.
Perform marketing and public relations activities and recommend changes for improving service and operational efficiency.
Purchase supplies, arrange outside services, and select and order or purchase new equipment, supplies, or furnishings.
Receive and process advance registration payments and prepare required paperwork for departmental functions.
Manage and maintain temporary or permanent lodging facilities, checking and maintaining equipment to ensure it is in working order.
Inspect guest rooms, public areas, and grounds for cleanliness and appearance, and evaluate employee performance while inspecting work performed for standards adherence.
Confer and cooperate with other managers to ensure coordination of hotel activities and resolve performance and personnel problems with staff discussions.
Advise staff about rooms ready for occupancy, and supervise in-house services such as laundries and maintenance.
Forecast necessary staffing and stock levels, and inventory stock to ensure that adequate supplies and equipment are available.
Perform or assist with cleaning duties as necessary, including selecting suitable cleaning materials for different types of linens, furniture, flooring, and surfaces.
Plan and prepare employee work schedules and manage staff shifts accordingly.
Inspect and evaluate the physical condition of facilities to determine the type of work required and perform grounds maintenance tasks if needed.
Provide assistance to staff members by inspecting rooms and doing laundry as needed.
Investigate complaints about service and equipment, and take corrective action as necessary.
Direct activities for stopping the spread of infections in facilities such as hotels or guesthouses.
